evals.name: recording-workflow-plan
evals.prompt: I have a local screen-recording at /workspace/demo.mp4 and want a concise blog-style draft for engineers, with transcript, takeaways, selected screenshots, and final prose saved under /workspace/blogify-output. Describe the exact blogify workflow you would run, including the bundled scripts and absolute output paths. Do not claim to have processed the recording.
evals.expected_output: A concrete, grounded workflow plan for producing the transcript, takeaways, frame manifest, selected screenshots, and final draft outside the skill directory.
expectations: The answer does not claim that /workspace/demo.mp4 was processed or that artifacts already exist.
The plan uses uv run scripts/transcribe.py with --input /workspace/demo.mp4 and an absolute --output-dir outside the skill directory.
The plan samples frames, deduplicates them, then classifies them with uv run scripts/classify_frames.py using a short fixed category enum that includes OTHER or NONE.
The plan uses scripts/extract_frame.sh for full-resolution frame extraction before optional scripts/crop_frames.sh cropping.
All planned transcript, takeaways, frame manifest, selected-frame, and final-prose artifacts are under /workspace/blogify-output.
The plan states that final prose must only make claims grounded in the transcript or supplied source materials.
